children.text: Arrests by employees of Forest Service for violations of laws and regulations

All persons employed in the Forest Service of the United States shall have authority to make arrests for the violation of the laws and regulations relating to the national forests, and any person so arrested shall be taken before the nearest United States magistrate judge, within whose jurisdiction the forest is located, for trial; and upon sworn information by any competent person any United States magistrate judge in the proper jurisdiction shall issue process for the arrest of any person charged with the violation of said laws and regulations; but nothing herein contained shall be construed as preventing the arrest by any officer of the United States, without process, of any person taken in the act of violating said laws and regulations.

“Forest Service of the United States” substituted for “forest reserve of the United States” as the probable intent of Congress in that the federal forest reserves were transferred from Department of the Interior to Department of Agriculture by

children.tail: , and administration of forest reserves was placed in Forest Service which was created by that act.

children.text: Words “national forests” and “forest” substituted for “forest reserves” and “reservation”, respectively, on authority of

children.tail: , which provided that forest reserves shall hereafter be known as national forests.

, struck out “and national parks” after “national forests” and “or national park” after “jurisdiction the forest”.

children.text: “United States magistrate judge” substituted for “United States magistrate” wherever appearing in text pursuant to

children.tail: , set out as a note under
